legal_basis: Finanzkonten-Informationsaustauschgesetz (FkAustG) vom 21. Dezember 2015 (BGBl. I S. 2531); Gemeinsamer Meldestandard-Umsetzungsgesetz (GMS-UmsG)
crs_status: Committed and active - first exchange 2017 (early adopter)
reporting_deadline: 31 July following the 31 December reporting year-end
submission_method: BZSt Online Portal (MOP); XML upload; ELSTER not used for CRS
xml_schema: OECD CRS XML Schema v2.0; BZSt publishes German-specific technical guidance
nil_report_required: Yes - nil report required if Reporting FI has no reportable accounts
data_retention_years: 6 years from end of reporting year (Abgabenordnung)
msg_ref_id_format: DE + Reporting Year + FI identifier + sequence; exact format in BZSt technical spec
doc_ref_id_format: DE + Reporting Year + FI tax number (Steuer-IdNr) + account unique reference
local_fi_scope: All German-resident credit institutions (Kreditinstitute) per KWG
German-resident life insurance companies with investment-linked products
German branches of foreign FIs resident in Germany for CRS purposes
excluded_accounts: Qualified credit card issuer accounts (where applicable)
Riester-Rente and Ruerup-Rente pension contracts (state-subsidised pensions)
Bausparvertraege (building savings contracts) registered with BZSt as non-reporting
local_specific_rules: 90-DAY SELF-CERT RULE WITH ACCOUNT BLOCK (UNIQUE TO GERMANY): Under FkAustG s.19, if a new account holder does not provide a valid self-certification within 90 days of account opening, the FI is required to BLOCK the account from processing withdrawals or fund transfers until self-cert is received. This is a unique German rule with no OECD equivalent.

ACCOUNT FREEZE ENFORCEMENT: The account block under the 90-day rule is legally binding. FI must implement a system flag to suspend outbound transactions on day 91 if self-cert is outstanding. Compliance teams must monitor and report freeze status.
NIL REPORTS: Mandatory in Germany unlike some other jurisdictions. FI must actively submit a nil report for years with no reportable accounts.

EU DAC2 OVERLAY: Germany applies EU Directive 2014/107/EU (DAC2) in addition to OECD CRS. For EU partner exchanges, DAC2 governs; for non-EU MCAA partners, OECD CRS governs. In practice the rules are identical.
SELF-CERT LANGUAGE: German language self-certification forms preferred by BZSt for German-based accounts, though OECD forms in English are accepted.
ELSTER EXCLUSION: Germany's standard tax filing system (ELSTER) is NOT used for CRS. BZSt Online Portal (MOP) is the mandatory channel.

ENTITY CLASSIFICATION - INVESTMENTVERMOGEN: German investment funds (Investmentvermogen per InvStG) have specific classification rules that differ slightly from OECD standard. Verify with BZSt technical guidance.
TECHNICAL GUIDANCE: BZSt publishes an annual 'Technisches Handbuch' (technical manual) for CRS XML submission. Always use the current year version.

rejection_error_codes.resolution: Validate against BZSt-published XSD; check mandatory elements
Use OECD2 with CorrDocRefId if amending

rejection_error_codes.desc: FI not registered in BZSt system
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Register FI with BZSt before first submission
Confirm ReportingPeriod element matches registered reporting year

rejection_error_codes.desc: Invalid TIN format for stated jurisdiction
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Review OECD TIN guidance; populate IssuedBy correctly

penalties.failure_to_report: Under AO s.379(2): fine up to EUR 25,000 per violation
penalties.account_block_violation: Administrative penalty for failure to implement account block within 90 days

fatca.portal: BZSt Online Portal (MOP) for FATCA reporting
key_rules: Germany Model 1A: German FIs report FATCA data to BZSt, which passes to IRS.
US persons identified by 7 standard FATCA indicia.
German FIs registered as FFIs on IRS FATCA registration portal for GIIN, but report via BZSt.

sources.notes: BZSt CRS procedure page - fetched and verified

gaps: BZSt rejection error code identifiers should be confirmed against latest BZSt Technisches Handbuch
Current year BZSt technical manual URL should be added when available
